stories.headline: Iran fires missiles at Israel for first time since April truce, hours after Israeli strike on Beirut
stories.summary: Iran launched waves of ballistic missiles at northern Israel late June 7 in retaliation for Israel's strike on Beirut's Dahieh, the first direct Iranian attack on Israel since the April 8 ceasefire. The IRGC said it targeted the Ramat David air base; the IDF said air defenses intercepted the missiles with no immediate casualties. Trump said the Israeli strikes were not coordinated with Washington, urged both sides to stand down, and insisted the barrage would not derail US-Iran talks.

perspectives.keyFraming: The escalation exposes the limits of Trump's diplomacy as Israel acts unilaterally and Iran retaliates, undercutting claims a deal is near.
perspectives.summary: Iran fired several barrages toward Israel on June 7 for the first time since April, after Israel's Beirut strike killed two and wounded 20, per Lebanon's health ministry. Trump said the US was 'very close to a final deal' and that he would call Netanyahu to urge no retaliation, while acknowledging the Israeli strikes were not coordinated with Washington.

sources.title: Israel says Iran launched missiles toward it after Beirut strikes
Iran fires waves of missiles into Israel for first time since April ceasefire

perspectives.keyFraming: A fragile, repeatedly violated ceasefire is in jeopardy as Iran's first direct fire on Israel since April collides with stalled US-brokered talks.
perspectives.summary: Israel said Iran launched missiles in the first bombardment since the early-April ceasefire, with explosions heard in northern Israel and air defenses reporting interceptions and no immediate casualties. Tehran had warned of retaliation after Israel struck Beirut's southern suburbs without warning; Trump pressed Iran to stop firing and return to talks.

sources.title: Israel says Iran launched a missile at it, in a first during fragile ceasefire
Israel says Iran launched missiles in first bombardment since fragile ceasefire

perspectives.keyFraming: Iran's barrages and threats against US targets show Tehran escalating, with Trump warning it to 'get back to the table.'
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning coverage framed Iran as unleashing missile barrages on Israel and threatening US targets in a sudden escalation, with Trump warning Tehran to return to negotiations and insisting a deal remains close, casting the president as pressing both sides to avoid a wider war.

sources.title: Iran unleashes missile barrages on Israel, threatens US targets in sudden escalation
Trump urges Iran to make a deal after Iran fires missiles at Israel

perspectives.keyFraming: Israel says it intercepted every missile and is weighing options as Iran threatens 'devastating' follow-on strikes.
perspectives.summary: The War Zone and Haaretz reported the IRGC's claim that it struck the Ramat David air base, Iran's warning of 'more forceful blows' if Israel responds, and the IDF statement that the missiles aimed at Israel were shot down. Hospitals in the north were ordered to shift to protected wards.

sources.title: Iran Launches Ballistic Missile Attacks On Israel (Updated)
Live: Iran Fires Missiles at Israel for First Time Since April Truce

stories.headline: Russian drone strikes Chornobyl spent-fuel storage facility; IAEA calls it 'deeply alarming,' radiation normal
stories.summary: A Russian drone struck the Centralized Spent Nuclear Fuel Storage Facility in the Chornobyl Exclusion Zone early June 7, partially destroying a container-reception building and sparking a fire that was extinguished within an hour. Energoatom said no spent fuel was stored in the hit building and radiation remained normal. Zelensky condemned the strike as terror; IAEA chief Grossi called it 'deeply alarming,' noting nuclear material was stored just metres away.

perspectives.keyFraming: A strike on a nuclear-storage site renews safety fears even as both sides' deep-strike drone war intensifies.
perspectives.summary: CBC and CBS reported a Russian drone damaged the spent-fuel storage building near Chornobyl, with Ukraine saying no fuel was hit and radiation normal; the IAEA, which inspected the site, called the incident 'deeply alarming.' The strike came amid wider overnight Russian attacks that killed three.

sources.title: Russian drone hit nuclear-fuel storage facility near Chornobyl, Ukraine says
Russian strikes kill 3, damage nuclear fuel storage site near Chernobyl, Ukraine says

perspectives.keyFraming: Kyiv brands the strike 'sky-high arrogance'; Energoatom and the SBU publish drone evidence.
perspectives.summary: The Kyiv Independent and Kyiv Post reported Energoatom's account of the container-reception building's partial destruction, the contained fire, and Zelensky's condemnation of the attack on critical nuclear infrastructure, with the SBU publishing imagery of the drone used.

sources.title: Russian drone strikes spent nuclear fuel storage in Chornobyl, Energoatom says
Russian Drone Strike Damages Chornobyl Nuclear Waste Storage Facility

stories.headline: Oil eases into the weekend as Iran sends updated peace proposal; Brent near $97, WTI near $91
stories.summary: Crude eased further as Iran sent an updated peace proposal to mediators, reviving hopes of a settlement that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz, effectively closed since late February. Brent settled near $93 and WTI near $90. On June 7 OPEC+ approved a fourth output-quota hike since the closure (+188,000 bpd from July), though analysts note the increase means little while the strait stays shut. Weak Chinese demand added pressure.

perspectives.keyFraming: Diplomatic progress is slowly pulling the war premium out of crude, but the Hormuz blockade keeps a floor under prices.
perspectives.summary: Oil retreated Friday on signs Iran is still engaging diplomatically, sending an updated proposal to mediators that buoyed hopes for a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. A tentative framework would extend the ceasefire 60 days, reopen the strait toll-free and lift the US port blockade. Softening Chinese demand reinforced the move, though analysts caution prices stay elevated until traffic normalizes.

sources.title: U.S. and Iranian negotiators reach tentative deal to extend ceasefire and start new nuclear talks
Current price of oil as of June 5, 2026

perspectives.keyFraming: OPEC+ supply discipline and the Hormuz chokepoint mean energy prices stay elevated even as headlines turn hopeful.
perspectives.summary: Despite Friday's dip, the structural picture stays tight: the strait has been effectively closed since late February and any ceasefire extension still awaits final approval. Until shipping normalizes, US consumers face elevated pump prices regardless of diplomatic optimism.

sources.title: OPEC+ approves fourth oil output quota hike since Strait of Hormuz closure
Oil prices turn lower as U.S.-Iran ceasefire extension awaits Trump approval

stories.headline: After House rebuke, Senate war-powers fight on Iran heads toward a floor showdown
stories.summary: The House's 215-208 June 3 passage of an Iran war-powers resolution pushes the fight to the Senate, where Leader Thune and Minority Leader Schumer are negotiating a time agreement for a motion to proceed. Schumer has pledged repeated war-powers votes until the conflict ends. A concurrent resolution would not require Trump's signature but generally lacks the force of law.

perspectives.keyFraming: Democrats argue Trump's 'war of choice' is unauthorized and failing, and vow to keep forcing votes.
perspectives.summary: Senate Democratic leadership framed the renewed push as building pressure to rein in an open-ended conflict, arguing the war raised gas prices, left Iran's nuclear program unverified and damaged US credibility, while insisting only a durable diplomatic settlement can end it.

sources.title: Schumer announces imminent vote on war powers resolution to rein in Trump's war in Iran
Schumer says Senate will vote on Iran war powers resolution next week
